creator: karels@freebsd.org
text:
I just retested this using a 14.0 installer on a system with an existing Linux (Ubuntu server) installation.  I used the manual partitioning mode, creating ufs and swap partitions.  The efi partition was not reformatted.  The installer asked about deleting duplicate FreeBSD entries, due to a previous installation that I had overwritten with Linux.  I deleted the duplicates, and am now able to boot from either FreeBSD or Linux.

I think this problem has been fixed in the meantime.  I intend to close this bug in a few weeks unless there is an objection.
